hero.title:
Extended Warranty
Program
hero.subtitle: Register your system and double your coverage — from 12 to 24 months of manufacturer warranty at no additional cost.

observation.title: Attention:
observation.description: The power battery is a consumable item and is not covered by the extended warranty program. The warranty also does not cover misuse, negligence, or incorrect installation.

form.title: Activate your extended warranty
form.subtitle: Fill out the form to secure 24 months of full coverage for your Fert Sensor system.

install-header.title: System installation and setup
install-header.subtitle: — User Guide
install-header.description: Follow the steps below to properly install and configure your Fert Sensor and AT200 Monitor.

install-section.title: 1- Installing the Fert Sensor on the implement
install-section.description: Before installing, choose the best position on the implement.

steps.description: Secure the sensor with the plastic clamp included with the product.
Pass the strap through the front channel of the sensor and wrap it around the conveyor tube.
Tighten it until the sensor is firmly fixed, without deforming the conveyor tube.
Test it by shaking the assembly — simulate the implement's movement.
Cut off the excess strap after fastening.
Make sure the sensors are aligned and positioned the same way.

observation.title: Attention:
observation.description: The sensor must not come into contact with any other part of the implement.

install-section.title: 2- Installing the AT200 Monitor on the tractor
install-section.description: Remember to install the monitor in a comfortable position for the operator.

steps.description: Clean the surface, attach the suction cup to the back of the monitor, press firmly, and turn the latch.
Point the monitor toward the operator's seat.
Keep the antenna always vertical.
Avoid placing it behind the cab pillars and near devices that may cause interference (such as radio communicators).

observation.title: Electrical connection:
observation.description: Connect the red cable (3A fuse) to the positive (+) terminal and the black cable to the negative (−) terminal of the tractor battery (12V DC). Avoid contact with moving parts or hot surfaces.

install-section.title: 3- Identification and addressing of the sensors
install-section.description: Each sensor must be identified with the row number and addressed to the monitor before operation.

steps.description: On the front of the sensor there are 3 fields — use a permanent marker and fill in the row number (e.g., 001, 002...).
On the monitor, press [ MENU ] → [ SENSOR ] → [ ADD ] .
Select the row number with ❨v❩ ❨˄❩ and confirm ❨✓❩.
Bring the sensor symbol close to the monitor symbol for ~5 seconds.
Wait for the audible and visual confirmation and repeat for each sensor.

observation.title: Note:
observation.description: If the sensor is not recognized, it may be in power-saving mode. Tap the sensor lightly to wake it up.

install-section.title: 4- Main screen and status reading
install-section.description: On the main screen of the AT200, you can monitor the status of all monitored rows in real time.

steps.description: FLOW OK (arrows >>>): all rows with normal flow.
MANEUVER: flow interrupted by a stop or maneuver of the implement.
FAILURE + row number: clogging or lack of material — check the indicated row.
LOW BATTERY: sensor battery needs to be replaced soon.

observation.title: Observation:
observation.description: The number in the lower-right corner indicates addressed and communicating rows.

install-section.title: 5- Menu settings
install-section.description: Press ❨X❩ on the main screen to access and exit the monitor settings menu.

steps.description: Language: [ MENU ] → [ SYSTEM / SISTEMA ] → [ LANGUAGE / IDIOMA ] — Change language (PT-BR/EN-US/ES-ES).
History: [ MENU ] → [ HISTORY ] — Show complete alarm log with date, time, and duration (recommended for diagnosing recurring issues).
Brightness/Volume: [ MENU ] → [ BRIGHTNESS / BEEP VOLUME ] → ❨v❩ ❨˄❩ → ❨✓❩ — 5 levels of adjustment.
Date/time: [ MENU ] → [ SYSTEM ] → [ DATE TIME ] — Adjust day, month, year, hour and minute.
Read sensor: [ MENU ] → [ SENSOR ] → [ READ ] — Verify the configured row and monitor.
Disable: [ MENU ] → [ SENSOR ] → [ DISABLE ] — Remove row from the network.
Battery Level: [ MENU ] → [ SYSTEM ] → [ BATT INFO. ] — Check battery level of all sensors.

observation.title: Hint:
observation.description: To cancel any operation, press ✕ — the monitor immediately returns to the main screen.

install-section.title: 6- Replacing the Fert Sensor battery
install-section.description: The monitor warns you in advance when it needs to be replaced.

steps.description: Remove the sensor from the implement
Using a Phillips screwdriver, loosen the 4 screws and remove the cover
Pull out the battery and foam to access the connector
Disconnect carefully by pulling on the connector, not the wire
Connect the new battery (the fit should be smooth, confirming correct alignment)
Make sure the sealing ring is in the proper place
Reinstall the foam, battery, cover, and screws
The sensor will automatically resume communication while keeping the configuration

observation.title: Important:
observation.description: Use original FertiSystem batteries. Damage to wires, connectors, or sealing during replacement is not covered by the warranty.

faq-header.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq-header.subtitle: — FAQ
faq-header.description: Choose a category to quickly find your answer.

questions.question: What do I get by registering my system?
questions.answer: Your warranty is extended from 12 to 24 months at no additional cost. Coverage includes manufacturing defects in the Fert Sensor and the AT200 Monitor, as long as installation, operation, and maintenance follow the manual instructions.

questions.question: Do I have a deadline to register?
questions.answer: Yes. Registration must be completed within 30 days after installation. After this period, the benefit may no longer be available.

questions.question: What happens if I do not register?
questions.answer: Your system keeps the standard 12-month warranty from the invoice date. You only lose the additional 12 months of the extended warranty.

questions.question: How do I make a warranty claim?
questions.answer: Contact Thunderstruck or J2M directly and provide the purchase invoice number. The warranty is depot/service-center type — the product must be sent in for inspection. Technicians do not make on-site visits for installations or repairs.

questions.question: What does the warranty cover and not cover?
questions.answer:
Covers:

  • Manufacturing defects in the Fert Sensor and AT200 Monitor
  • Component failures under normal use conditions

Does not cover:

  • Power battery (consumable item)
  • Incorrect installation or installation outside the manual standards
  • Misuse, negligence, or lack of maintenance
  • Disassembly or repair by an unauthorized person
  • External damage caused by the user
  • Natural wear of components

questions.question: Which application types are compatible?
questions.answer:
The system is compatible with Gravity, Air Drill, and Strip Till.
  • For Gravity: Use the sensor with the attached clamp.
  • For Air Drill and Strip Till: The sensor does not have an attached clamp.

questions.question: Which conveyor tubes are compatible?
questions.answer: Conveyor tubes from 25mm to 100mm in diameter. The recommended types are corrugated and hose. Rigid plastic tubes are not recommended.

questions.question: Does the sensor need cables or charging?
questions.answer: No. The Fert Sensor is completely wireless. It communicates via 915 MHz RF and is powered by a non-rechargeable lithium battery (3.6V – 4000mAh), with an approximate service life of 2,500 hours of use.

questions.question: How many sensors does the AT200 support?
questions.answer: The AT200 Monitor supports up to 120 sensors per monitor, communicating exclusively with FertiSystem sensors via 915 MHz RF.

questions.question: Does the monitor work on tractors without a cab?
questions.answer: Yes. The AT200 can be installed on tractors with or without a cab. See the installation recommendations in the manual for the correct setup in each case.

questions.question: What is the power supply voltage?
questions.answer: The monitor operates on 12V DC, connected directly to the tractor battery. The 5m power cable comes with the product, with an external 3A blade fuse.

questions.question: Is the monitor rain resistant?
questions.answer: Yes. The AT200 Monitor has IP54 protection — resistant to water splashes from any direction. It is not intended for immersion or direct jets.

questions.question: What does "MANEUVER" mean on the screen?
questions.answer: "MANEUVER" indicates interrupted flow in all or many rows at the same time — usually due to a stop, a turn at the end of the field, or refilling. It is not a failure alarm. When flow resumes, the monitor will display "FLOW OK" again.

questions.question: What should I do when "FAILURE" appears?
questions.answer: The indicated row has no flow while the others are normal. Check the corresponding row: there may be a clog in the conveyor tube or a lack of material in that row's meter.

questions.question: Does the sensor with "LOW BATTERY" stop monitoring?
questions.answer: Not immediately. The alert gives you advance notice. The sensor continues operating for a while, but it is important to replace it so you do not lose row monitoring.

questions.question: Can I use it with any granular input?
questions.answer: Yes. The Fert Sensor was designed to monitor granular materials in general — fertilizers, cover-crop seeds, and other inputs.

form-reminder.title: Ready to register?
form-reminder.subtitle: It takes less than 5 minutes — and doubles your coverage.
